title: PRISM team config
description: Per-team config for token substitution and tech-stack-conditional content. Lives at .ai-skills/config.json in the consumer's repo. The onboarding flow (Atlas, Phase 2) writes this; agents read from it at chat time when behavior depends on tech stack. Derived tokens used at sync time but not stored as fields: ${PROJECT_LOWERCASE} (lowercase form of project, used for git-config namespaces), ${TICKET_PREFIX_LOWERCASE} (lowercase form of ticketPrefix). Add new derived tokens at the same seam in scripts/ai-skills/lib/tokens.ts (Phase 2).

org.description: Display org name. Used in PR descriptions, changelog headers, etc.

ticketPrefix.description: Ticket ID prefix used in branch names, commits, and PR titles.

ticketSystem.description: Ticket system configuration. `kind: "linear"` and `kind: "github-issues"` are supported; the object shape is the abstraction lever for future providers (e.g. Jira). The Linear-specific fields below apply only when `kind` is `linear`.

kind.description: Ticket system provider. `linear` carries workspace/teamKey; `github-issues` tracks work as GitHub issues with no Linear team.

projectId.description: Linear project UUID, if scoped to a single project.

teamKey.description: Linear team key. Often matches ticketPrefix.

github.description: GitHub repo info for Eric (PR review), Sage (changelog), Reese (QA plans), Lilac (standup).

defaultBranch.description: Default branch name. Most teams use `main`.

techStack.description: Tech stack flags. Drives onboarding's per-codebase rule generation — for each language and framework listed, Atlas generates a corresponding rules file populated from patterns in the team's actual code (Phase 2). Validated against an enum so the set stays curated; add new values to the enum when a real team needs them.

commands.description: Canonical verification commands for this team. Atlas populates this map during onboarding from the detected tech stack; skill prose and verification-commands.md reference these values as {{commands.*}} tokens so command strings live in one place. Each value is the full shell command to run from the repo root.

typecheck.description: Command that runs the TypeScript type-checker (or equivalent for the team's language). Exit 0 means types pass.

test.description: Command that runs the full test suite. Exit 0 means tests pass.

lint.description: Command that runs the linter (ESLint, crossref-lint, or equivalent). Exit 0 means lint passes.

format.description: Command that runs the formatter in check mode (Prettier --check, or equivalent). Empty string when the team has no standalone formatter step.

build.description: Command that runs the full build. Exit 0 means the build passes.

additionalProperties.description: Additional team-specific command slots (e.g. 'e2e', 'storybook'). Atlas may add these during onboarding.

rules.description: Rule selection. `universal` is the always-included set (the universal rule set ships with PRISM). Language- and framework-specific rules are generated per-codebase during onboarding (Phase 2), not opted into via this field. `optIn` is reserved for future non-language opt-in rules.

optIn.description: Names of opt-in rule files (without .md extension).

slackChannel.description: Optional Slack channel for Lilac standup posting.

documentation.description: Documentation configuration. Populated by Atlas during onboarding (Phase 2). Eli reads these values to write guides shaped for the team's actual doc system — the format field in particular drives which patterns Eli uses. format is an open string, never a closed union: a new consumer adds their value here without a schema migration.

location.description: Root path where the team's user-facing docs live, relative to the repo root.

audience.description: Primary audience for the docs. Use `developer-user` when the repo itself is a developer tool (the reader is also a builder). Other values are open — Atlas writes whatever the team's onboarding answer produces.

keepsDevDocs.description: Whether the team maintains technical/dev docs separate from user-facing docs. When true, Eli's paired-doc workflow is active (prism-doc-walker steps 05/06/07). When false, all docs are user-facing and the paired-doc flow is skipped.

format.description: Doc format the team uses. Open string — not a closed union. Atlas writes the value that matches the team's actual setup; Eli adapts its output to the declared format. Known values: `flat-markdown-guides` (PRISM), `nextra-blocks` (Thrive). New consumers add their own value here without a schema migration.

features.description: Feature flags. Read by specific personas only; not surfaced during init or onboarding. Set manually after install.

conductorMayMerge.description: When true, Sol (the Conductor) may merge PRs when the Briar→Eric review loop is clean. Absent or false by default — merge remains a human responsibility. Read only by Sol; never mentioned in init or onboarding output.

retroEvidence.description: Per-team execution-record sources for Iris's retro charter (prism-retro step-02). Populated by Atlas's retro-evidence onboarding question set. A source this block marks absent renders in the retro's charter-coverage table as 'not configured for this team', distinct from a configured-but-unreachable-this-run source — so the coverage table degrades honestly on teams without CI or without a GitHub-hosted PR platform.

ci.description: Whether this team runs CI, and which system. Absent or `present: false` means charter item 6 (test-passing / CI record) always renders as not-configured.

system.description: CI system name. Open string — Atlas writes whatever the team names.

prPlatform.description: Platform hosting PRs/reviews. Drives how step-02 fetches PR review threads for charter items 4/5. Open string — not limited to GitHub.

testCommand.description: The team's test command, for reference in the retro's CI-conclusion reads. Mirrors commands.test — kept as a separate field so retroEvidence stays self-contained even if commands.* changes shape later.

dodGates.description: The gates that define 'done' for this team (types, lint, tests, build, coverage, etc.) — informs which charter items step-02 can expect evidence for.

modelTiers.description: Optional per-persona model selection for Sol's conductor dispatches. Maps PRISM's abstract tiers (top / worker, defined in prism-conductor shared.md) to this runtime's concrete model names, plus optional per-persona overrides. Read by Sol to seed each lane's goal-state `models` map; absent means the conductor uses its run-wide default model for every dispatch. Model-name strings are examples only — a consumer on a non-Claude runtime supplies that runtime's model identifiers.

top.description: Model for the top tier (Sol, Winston, Eric, and any escalated worker).

worker.description: Model for the worker tier (Clove, Sasha, Briar, and other default worker personas).

overrides.description: Optional per-persona model pins keyed by lowercase persona name (e.g. `eric`, `winston`). An override wins over the persona's default tier — the seam for cross-model review, where a team pins Eric to a different family than the workers. A value here is a concrete model name, not a tier name.
